paxton-ken.text: Ken Paxton is a candidate for United States Senator from Texas, running with Republican Party (United States).

On energy, Ken Paxton's recorded position: Paxton has strongly advocated for fossil fuel expansion, fighting to reinstate the Keystone XL pipeline, criticizing EPA rules targeting oil and gas, opposing FERC regulations on natural gas emissions, and arguing that 'green energy' cannot substitute for coal, nuclear, oil, and gas.
Ken Paxton's recorded background: Ken Paxton, attorney general of Texas (2015–present).
As of 2026-07-04, Ken Paxton's campaign had raised $7,605,209 in campaign receipts and spent $5,257,746, per FEC filings.

talarico-james.text: James Talarico is a candidate for United States Senator from Texas, running with Democratic Party (United States).

On taxes, James Talarico's recorded position: Talarico explicitly calls for raising taxes on billionaires and corporations, including higher taxes on income and capital gains, ending 'buy, borrow, die' loopholes, raising corporate tax rates, and closing carried interest loopholes to fund working family tax cuts.
James Talarico's recorded background: James Talarico, state representative from the 50th district (2018–present).
As of 2026-07-04, James Talarico's campaign had raised $40,284,109 in campaign receipts and spent $30,425,244, per FEC filings.

base.summary: United States Senator from Texas race: Ken Paxton, James Talarico. This overview lists each candidate's party, background, and campaign finance where available in our data set.
base.caveats: Our data set for this race covers candidate name, party, incumbency, background summary, and (for federal candidates only) FEC campaign finance totals. We do not have recorded voting histories or issue-by-issue policy positions for these candidates in this data set; state executive offices generally do not file with the FEC, so campaign finance is not available for most of them here.

renter_young_worker.summary: For a renter young worker voter looking at the United States Senator from Texas race (Ken Paxton, James Talarico): our data set does not contain candidate-specific positions or voting records on housing costs, rent, or renters' protections for this race. Below is what we do have on each candidate's background and, where available, campaign finance.
renter_young_worker.caveats: We only show a connection to renter young worker concerns when a candidate's own recorded background in our data set names it directly (for example, a farming or small-business background). Where no such record exists, we say so rather than guess at a candidate's likely position.

talarico-james.text: On taxes, James Talarico's recorded position: Talarico explicitly calls for raising taxes on billionaires and corporations, including higher taxes on income and capital gains, ending 'buy, borrow, die' loopholes, raising corporate tax rates, and closing carried interest loopholes to fund working family tax cuts.

On education, James Talarico's recorded position: Talarico opposes private school vouchers and has made opposition to school choice diversion programs central to his campaign. He supports universal pre-K, capping class sizes, and increasing public school funding.

As of 2026-07-04, James Talarico's campaign had raised $40,284,109 in total, per FEC filings. That is general campaign-finance context; it does not tell us the candidate's stance on property taxes or public school funding.

talarico-james.text: On healthcare, James Talarico's recorded position: Talarico supports allowing every American regardless of age to join Medicare as a public option, creating government-guaranteed coverage available to all while preserving private insurance competition. He has legislated to cap insulin costs and import cheaper drugs from Canada.

As of 2026-07-04, James Talarico's campaign had raised $40,284,109 in total, per FEC filings. That is general campaign-finance context; it does not tell us the candidate's stance on health coverage, the ACA, or Medicaid.

talarico-james.text: On social security, James Talarico's recorded position: Talarico supports expanding Social Security benefits and funding them by eliminating the payroll tax cap on earnings over $400,000 annually. He opposes benefit cuts and privatization.

On medicare medicaid, James Talarico's recorded position: Talarico supports expanding Medicare eligibility to all ages as a public option and protecting Medicaid from cuts. He has opposed legislation that would kick people off Medicaid coverage.

As of 2026-07-04, James Talarico's campaign had raised $40,284,109 in total, per FEC filings. That is general campaign-finance context; it does not tell us the candidate's stance on Medicare, Social Security, or retiree benefits.
